checks.finding: Artifacts, modes, interfaces, validation rules, states, and outputs are concrete; category-level Japanese and English probe counts and non-pairing are explicit.
Requirements, acceptance criteria, non-goals, and behavioral contracts are mutually consistent.

Acceptance tests cover material negative and boundary paths including unaffected layers, absent mockups, legacy specs, missing files, invalid slugs, tampering, invalid traceability, and approval states.
Validated decisions resolve diagram rendering, review ownership, version baseline, checker interface, and review surface; remaining risks have planned validation.
Human or active signed-sudo approval, Draft persistence, Draft rejection, and authorized transition are observable.
Artifact inventories, template content, command interfaces, traceability syntax, hash binding, compatibility, release metadata, and verification targets are explicit.
